name: Patriot Equity
description_short: Patriot Equity Credit Union is a federally insured state-chartered credit union in Jackson, TN with $33.5M in assets, serving 2,207 members since 1929.
description_long:
Patriot Equity Credit Union was established in 1929 and has operated for 97 years as a federally insured, state-chartered credit union headquartered in Jackson, Tennessee. The institution is regulated under charter number 60082 and maintains membership eligibility for state-chartered natural persons. With a single branch location on Union University Drive, the credit union serves a membership base of approximately 2,207 individuals and employs 7 full and part-time staff members.

The credit union offers standard consumer banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM and debit card programs, and various loan products. Their loan portfolio includes real estate loans ($9.7M), unsecured personal loans ($496K), and other loan categories totaling $20.6M in outstanding loans. The institution maintains $33.5M in total assets with $29.75M in member shares and deposits, indicating strong member confidence and capitalization.

Patriot Equity distinguishes itself as a community-focused, member-owned financial cooperative with deep roots in West Tennessee. As a credit union rather than a commercial bank, it operates on a not-for-profit basis returning earnings to members through competitive rates on savings and loans. The organization is not a corporate credit union and does not hold FHLB membership, operating as an independent community institution.

However, the credit union reported a net loss of $53,987 in its most recent audit (as of December 1, 2024), indicating operational challenges. With limited branch presence (1 location) and a relatively small membership base compared to larger regional institutions, the credit union may have constraints on service offerings and digital banking capabilities. Prospective members should verify current interest rates and available services directly, as smaller credit unions sometimes offer more limited product ranges than larger competitors. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Real estate loans (mortgages and home loans)
Direct deposit capability via ABA routing number
Member account management at main office location
pros: Federally insured deposits through NCUSIF, protecting member accounts up to $250,000
97-year operating history demonstrates long-term stability and community commitment since 1929
Member-owned not-for-profit structure returns earnings to members rather than shareholders
Low-cost wire transfer services available to members
ABA routing number 284383954 enables standard banking transfers and direct deposits
Maintains low employee compensation ratio ($169K for 7 staff), suggesting operational efficiency
Real estate loan portfolio ($9.7M) indicates mortgage and home financing options available
cons: Reported net loss of $53,987 in most recent period (Dec 2024 audit) raises profitability concerns
Single branch location limits physical access and in-person service options for members
Small membership base (2,207 members) may restrict service offerings and competitive rates compared to larger institutions
No FHLB membership limits access to certain funding and liquidity resources available to larger credit unions
Loan portfolio concentration risk with $10.2M in 'other loans' category (undefined composition)
best_for: West Tennessee residents near Jackson seeking local, community-based banking relationships
Members prioritizing credit union membership and not-for-profit banking philosophy
Individuals with existing family or community ties to Jackson and Union University area
Borrowers seeking real estate loans or unsecured personal loans with local underwriting
diagnosis: Patriot Equity Credit Union is best suited for West Tennessee residents seeking a community-based, member-owned financial institution with traditional banking services and real estate financing. The primary caveat is the credit union's recent operational losses and single-branch limitation, which may impact service depth and competitive rates compared to larger regional or national credit unions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
